note: The correct half of the tie-order pair. reverse=True sorts descending while leaving tied runs in their original order, which is exactly why it exists instead of reversing afterwards. The two versions differ by a handful of characters and behave differently only on ties, so this pair is the sharpest discrimination test in the set. Every claim below was verified by running it.

labels.text: players are ordered from the highest score to the lowest
players with equal scores keep the order they were given in
labels.why: reverse=True inverts the comparison rather than the finished list, so stability is preserved. Verified: alice and bob, both on 3 and given in that order, come back as ['alice', 'bob', 'carol'].
